rules: Inter-agent communication should use English by default.
Specialized agents communicating with models should use the caveman skill by default.
Human interaction must adapt to the human's language.
Human-facing artifacts must follow the human's language or project context.
Do not use caveman compression with humans unless explicitly requested.

economical.description: Low-cost model profile for simple, low-risk and repetitive tasks.
notes: Use for simple documentation edits, formatting and low-risk context maintenance.

fast.description: Fast-response profile for routing, classification and simple coordination.
notes: Use for routing decisions after the required context is already clear.

standard.description: Balanced model profile for common planning, writing and coordination tasks.
notes: Use when the task needs reasoning but is not high-risk.

reasoning.description: Stronger reasoning profile for planning, architecture, dependencies and complex decisions.
notes: Use for specs, wave/task planning, architecture and ambiguous technical work.

high_assurance.description: Highest-accuracy profile for security, acceptance, final review and high-risk decisions.
notes: Use when mistakes are expensive or difficult to detect.

product-owner.rationale: Product and project setup decisions affect downstream planning.

env-configr.rationale: Environment and platform setup affects agent recruitment, skills, model routing and communication rules.

tech-lead.rationale: Planning requires dependency reasoning and scope control.

orchestrator.rationale: Coordination usually needs balanced reasoning; escalate when scope or risk is unclear.

spec-writer.rationale: Specs must preserve intent and avoid scope expansion.

context-maintainer.rationale: Most context updates are structured, but ambiguity may require escalation.

agent-recruiter.rationale: Recruiting agents requires stack, environment and responsibility analysis.

skill-builder.rationale: Skill creation requires research synthesis and safety review.

architecture-specialist.rationale: Architecture mistakes are expensive and affect many tasks.

ux-specialist.rationale: UX review is usually balanced, with escalation for complex flows.

review-specialist.rationale: Final review should optimize correctness over cost.

testing-specialist.rationale: Test planning requires coverage and risk reasoning.

acceptance-specialist.rationale: Acceptance validates whether the delivered result is actually ready.

cybersecurity-specialist.rationale: Security review should not optimize primarily for cost.

docs-maintainer.rationale: Documentation maintenance is often low risk unless it changes behavior or policy.

escalate_to_high_assurance_when: data loss or migration risk exists
release or production behavior is affected
public API compatibility is at risk
prefer_economical_when: task is documentation-only and low risk
task is formatting or summarization of already-approved content
no product, architecture, security, data or release decision is involved
require_human_confirmation_when: selected model is unavailable in the user's subscription
cost-sensitive work requires a stronger profile
task risk suggests escalation but no high-assurance model is configured

notes: Fill model names according to the Claude subscription available to the project.
Map profiles to provider/model IDs supported by the OpenCode configuration.
GitHub Copilot may not expose per-agent model routing in every plan. Keep this file as guidance when direct routing is unavailable.
Fill model names according to the Codex/OpenAI environment available to the project.
